title: A Distributed Gaussian Process Model for Multi-Robot Mapping
application_domain: Multi-Robot Mapping, Environmental Monitoring, Occupancy Mapping
approach: A peer-to-peer multi-robot algorithm using sparse GPs with inducing points as local summaries. It employs a loopy factor graph model for inference, relaxing the tree constraint of prior models (TSGPs) to improve accuracy and handle dynamic, asynchronous communication.
key_innovations: Relaxing the tree constraint of Tree-Structured GPs (TSGPs) to allow cycles in the communication graph.
Using a loopy model that is better suited for dynamic and asynchronous multi-robot settings.
Enforcing inter-robot consistency locally, where robots have overlapping data, rather than globally.
advantages_over_prior_work: Outperforms TSGPs in accuracy by allowing direct connections between inducing points that are close in Euclidean space.
More suitable for dynamic scenarios where communication patterns change, unlike TSGP which requires fixed connectivity.
Scalable, as each robot stores only a local map estimate.
contributions: A new distributed GP model, trainable with GBP, which outperforms TSGPs [4].
Empirical analysis showing the model can be built on-the-fly and admits distributed, asynchronous inference via GBP. It is thus well-suited to multi-robot mapping.
Experimental evaluation of the method on environmental monitoring and occupancy mapping simulations, showing superior performance to DiNNO [22].
related_work: Other distributed implicit neural maps using CADMM or direct regularisation [22, 8, 2, 23, 24]
